title: Financial Account Closure Checklist
summary: Working through bank, credit, and other financial accounts methodically.

tasks.title: List every known account
tasks.notes: Check mail and statements for accounts you didn't know about.

tasks.title: Identify which accounts are joint (pass to co-owner) vs. individual (go through the estate)
tasks.jurisdictionNote: Exact process depends on state law and account titling.

tasks.title: Check for payable-on-death (POD) or transfer-on-death (TOD) designations
Notify each bank with a certified death certificate
tasks.documents: Death certificate, proof of executor authority.

tasks.title: Confirm what proof of authority each institution specifically requires
Update — don't necessarily cancel — auto and home insurance
tasks.explanation: to reflect the new named insured.

tasks.title: Confirm your actual relationship to any debt
tasks.explanation: (authorized user vs. joint holder) before paying anything personally.

tasks.title: Request written verification before engaging with any debt collector
Route legitimate estate debt through the estate
tasks.explanation: not personal funds, unless you're a joint account holder.
